# Prism

> Prism is a Python CLI and library that statically scans Ansible roles, collections, and repositories to generate README-style documentation in Markdown, HTML, JSON, and PDF.

Use this file as the entry point for LLM context loading. Start with the main README and API/CLI sources, then use docs and fixtures for behavior details.

Prism uses static file analysis, so runtime-only Ansible behavior may remain unresolved and is intentionally reported as uncertain metadata.

## Documentation

- [Main README](README.md): Primary overview, current capabilities, CLI usage, library usage, and limitations.
- [Changelog](docs/CHANGELOG.md): Chronological record of implemented features and fixes.
- [Contributing Guide](docs/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contributor workflow and repository expectations.
- [Code of Conduct](docs/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Community and participation standards.
- [Roadmap TODO](docs/TODO.md): Planned work and remaining implementation gaps.
- [Coverage Workoff Plan](docs/COVERAGE_WORKOFF_PLAN.md): Coverage status and test expansion priorities.

## Specifications and Operational Notes

- [Pipeline Diagram](docs/pipeline-diagram.md): End-to-end flow of scanning and README generation.
- [CI Starter Workflows](docs/ci-starter-workflows.md): Reusable GitHub Actions templates for docs generation and validation.
- [Provenance Tracking](docs/PROVENANCE_TRACKING.md): Notes about variable provenance and uncertainty handling.
- [Feedback Integration](docs/FEEDBACK_INTEGRATION.md): How learn-system feedback is incorporated into scanner output.

## Core Interfaces

- [CLI Entrypoint](src/prism/cli.py): Command interface (`prism role`, `prism collection`, `prism repo`, `prism completion`).
- [Public API](src/prism/api.py): Stable programmatic entry points (`scan_role`, `scan_repo`, `scan_collection`).
- [Scanner Engine](src/prism/scanner.py): Core static analysis for tasks, variables, metadata, and runbook/catalog outputs.
- [Style Guide Engine](src/prism/style_guide.py): Style README parsing, heading mapping, and section preservation rules.
- [Policy Loader](src/prism/pattern_config.py): Pattern/policy config layering and remote-cache loading behavior.
- [Output Layer](src/prism/output.py): Output format selection and write behavior.

## Configuration and Tooling

- [Project Metadata](pyproject.toml): Package metadata, dependencies, and console script registration.
- [Tox Configuration](tox.ini): Test and coverage execution environments.
- [Tool Configuration](setup.cfg): Pytest/coverage and lint-related settings.
- [Development Requirements](requirements-dev.txt): Additional dependencies for local development.
- [Workflow: Prism Docs](.github/workflows/prism.yml): CI job that generates docs using the subcommand CLI.
- [Workflow: Main CI](.github/workflows/default.yml): Main CI checks for tests and quality gates.

## Examples and Fixtures

- [Fixture Index](src/prism/tests/roles/README.md): Catalog of role fixtures used by scanner and rendering tests.
- [Enhanced Mock Role](src/prism/tests/roles/enhanced_mock_role/README.md): Rich fixture role used for realistic scanner behavior.
- [Base Mock Role](src/prism/tests/roles/base_mock_role/README.md): Minimal fixture for baseline scan behavior.
- [In-Role Config Fixture](src/prism/tests/roles/inrole_config_role/README.md): Fixture that validates `.prism.yml`-driven rendering behavior.

## Optional

- [Style Guide Source Catalog](docs/STYLE_GUIDE_SOURCES.md): Candidate upstream README sources for style-fidelity testing.
- [Template: README](src/prism/templates/README.md.j2): Default Jinja template used for generated markdown output.
- [Template: Style Guide Source](src/prism/templates/STYLE_GUIDE_SOURCE.md): Bundled fallback style-guide source template.
- [Companion Project](https://github.com/mutl3y/prism-learn): Optional learning-loop orchestration and persistence layer.
